faqs.question: Why are rats common in Lenox Hill buildings?
faqs.answer: Lenox Hill's combination of older pre-war buildings with basement vulnerabilities, proximity to Central Park, dense restaurant scene, and aging subway infrastructure creates ideal rat habitats. Ground-floor commercial spaces, trash from high foot traffic areas, and gaps in building foundations provide both food sources and shelter for rat populations.
